steps.title: Verify the right angle and use correspondence
steps.content: Check the Pythagorean theorem: $20^2 + 48^2 = 400 + 2304 = 2704 = 52^2$. This means $RS^2 + ST^2 = TR^2$, so the right angle is at $S$. Since triangle $RST$ is similar to triangle $UVW$ with $T$ corresponding to $W$, angle $T$ in $RST$ equals angle $W$ in $UVW$. Therefore, $\tan W = \tan T$.

steps.title: Calculate tan T
steps.content:
In right triangle $RST$ with right angle at $S$, for angle $T$:
Opposite side $= RS = 20$, Adjacent side $= ST = 48$
$$\tan T = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} = \frac{20}{48} = \frac{5}{12}$$ Since $\tan W = \tan T$, that matches choice B — B is correct.
